response.justification: Strong general software engineer with 20 years of diverse experience and recent AI exposure, but critically misses the core requirement: recognized expertise in AI with demonstrated products or platforms built with AI. Current AI work at re:cinq focuses on agent infrastructure tooling rather than strategic AI/ML platform building. Experience trajectory shows individual contributor and staff roles rather than the director-level strategic technical leadership needed to advise enterprises on cloud/AI transformation and identify future state of cloud products.
strengths: 20 years of continuous software engineering across web, mobile, infrastructure, and embedded systems demonstrating strong technical foundation and adaptability
Current AI-agent infrastructure role at re:cinq shows emerging expertise in modern AI tooling (MCP, frameworks) aligned with agentic workflows
Proven ability to inspire and educate through workshops, conference talks, and open-source contributions (Trial, Fluent-Asserts, GISCollective)
concerns: No demonstrated expertise building AI/ML products or platforms—recent AI work is infrastructure/tooling focused, not strategic product development or ML platform strategy
No advanced degree in AI-related field; background is general computer science without specialized AI/ML training (preferred qualification)
Career shows individual contributor and staff engineer roles rather than strategic technical leadership at director level needed to advise customers on cloud/AI adoption

key_factors.factor: Strategic leadership gap for director-level role
